title: Allow customizing pod hostname
body:
* set hostname in pod yaml file
* set --hostname in pod create command

Fixes https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3732

Follow up PR of: https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/3818

Signed-off-by: Chen Zhiwei <zhiweik@gmail.com>

comments.body:
Hi @chenzhiwei. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) or [openshift](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

LGTM

Should we add an option to podman pod create --hostname foobar?

I have no idea on the question.  @haircommander @baude PTAL

@rhatdan good idea, will update this pr to add the --hostname.

Maybe need another PR to generate yaml with hostname.
Nice and clean PR. if possible i'd like a test added, and to drop host network for now, otherwise LGTM
Will update this PR to address all your comments.

If I add a container to a pod, should we block the ability to set the hostname in the container command IE
```
# podman create --pod foo --hostname bar
Error, pod has a hostname, and containers are not allowed to modify them...
```

I think uts=container should probably conflict with it already after what
@haircommander did for UTS namespaces

On Sun, Aug 18, 2019, 06:33 Daniel J Walsh <notifications@github.com> wrote:

Can we simply make `--pod` and `--hostname` conflict? Which means we don't allow running these two flags together.

When adding a container to a pod, podman should not allow setting container hostname since all containers in a pod share the infra container hostname.
It is possible to cobfigure the pod to share different (or no) namespaces. We should not allow setting a hostname if the pod is set to share UTS

But for Kubernetes, seems there is no way to make the containers in a pod to use different hostnames.

https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.15/#podspec-v1-core

/approve
/lgtm
Lets handle the conflict in a different PR.  This one looks good.
